type_definitions.name: TypeDefinitionKind
type_definitions.description: M1+S1: Pre-existing domain enum. M1 adds expected_methods: Vec<MethodDeclaration> to all 9 struct-based variants (Typestate, ValueObject, UseCase, Interactor, Dto, Command, Query, Factory, SecondaryAdapter). S1 adds new DomainService variant with both expected_members and expected_methods. After this track the enum has 15 variants total (was 14). TypeGraph/baseline schema is unchanged per CN-07 because TypeNode::members and TypeNode::methods already exist.

type_definitions.name: CatalogueLinterRuleKind
type_definitions.description: S3: New domain enum — discriminator for the 3 primitive linter rule kinds. FieldEmpty enforces that a specified field on a specified kind is an empty collection. FieldNonEmpty enforces that a specified field is non-empty. KindLayerConstraint enforces that a kind is only declared in permitted layers. Used as the tag in CatalogueLinterRule and reported in CatalogueLintViolation.

type_definitions.name: CatalogueLinterRule
type_definitions.description: S3: New domain value object — a single linter rule primitive. Carries rule_kind (CatalogueLinterRuleKind), the target kind tag string (e.g. 'value_object'), the target field name (e.g. 'expected_methods'), and optionally a set of permitted layer ids (for KindLayerConstraint). Constructed via a validating constructor that rejects empty target_kind strings, empty field names on FieldEmpty/FieldNonEmpty rules, and empty permitted_layers on KindLayerConstraint rules. Fields are private; read access via accessor methods (rule_kind / target_kind / target_field / permitted_layers) per project encapsulation convention. No serde in domain layer.

type_definitions.name: CatalogueLinterRuleError
type_definitions.description: S3: New domain error type — validation error for CatalogueLinterRule construction. EmptyTargetKind rejects blank kind tag strings. EmptyTargetField rejects blank field name strings on FieldEmpty/FieldNonEmpty rules. EmptyPermittedLayers rejects KindLayerConstraint rules with no permitted layers (would forbid all placements).

type_definitions.name: CatalogueLintViolation
type_definitions.description: S3: New domain value object — a single lint violation produced when a CatalogueLinterRule fires against a TypeCatalogueEntry. Carries the rule kind, the entry name, and a human-readable message. Output element of the linter evaluation. All fields are private, accessed via accessors (rule_kind / entry_name / message) per project encapsulation convention.

type_definitions.name: CatalogueLinterError
type_definitions.description: S3: New domain error type — covers linter execution errors (distinct from CatalogueLintViolation which is findings output). InvalidRuleConfig: a rule could not be applied because its configuration is internally inconsistent at evaluation time. Used as the Err arm of the CatalogueLinter port's run method.

type_definitions.name: CatalogueLinter
type_definitions.description: S3: New domain secondary port (driven port) — the interface that a catalogue linter adapter must implement. The run method evaluates a slice of CatalogueLinterRule instances against a TypeCatalogueDocument and returns a Vec<CatalogueLintViolation> on success. The layer_id parameter provides the layer context needed for KindLayerConstraint rules (the catalogue document itself does not carry layer identity; it is injected by the caller). The CLI subcommand (bin/sotp track lint or similar) drives this port through an interactor in the usecase layer.
